title: Listing networks via Docker compat is far slower than Docker
body:
### Issue Description

Listing networks via the docker compat support is disproportionally slower than docker's support. 
In our case, it's slow enough (becomes multiple seconds) that we may have to switch to docker.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Test Case: 

Requires python 3.11 and python packages:
* pytest
* pytest-mocker
* docker

```python
import contextlib
import dataclasses
import os
import tempfile
import time
import typing

import docker


def get_client():
    return docker.from_env()


@dataclasses.dataclass
class Dind:
    image: str
    cmd: typing.Optional[list[str]]


@contextlib.contextmanager
def dind_factory(dind: Dind):
    tmp_folder = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()

    client = docker.from_env()
    container = client.containers.run(
        dind.image,
        dind.cmd,
        detach=True,
        privileged=True,
        user=os.getuid(),
        volumes=[f"{tmp_folder.name}:/sockets"],
        environment={"XDG_RUNTIME_DIR": "/sockets"},
        remove=True,
    )

    while True:
        try:
            dind_client = docker.from_env(timeout=1)
            dind_client.images.pull("quay.io/podman/hello")
            dind_client.containers.run("quay.io/podman/hello")
        except Exception:
            continue
        break

    try:
        yield f"unix://{tmp_folder.name}/docker.sock"
    finally:
        # Clear up Containers
        container.remove(force=True)
        tmp_folder.cleanup()


def test_network_list_performance(mocker):
    def test():
        client = get_client()
        client.images.pull("docker.io/library/busybox:latest")

        for i in range(25):
            client.networks.create(f"net{i}")
            client.containers.run(
                "docker.io/library/busybox:latest",
                ["sleep", "100000"],
                network=f"net{i}",
                detach=True,
            )
            client.containers.run(
                "docker.io/library/busybox:latest",
                ["sleep", "100000"],
                network=f"net{i}",
                detach=True,
            )
            client.containers.run(
                "docker.io/library/busybox:latest",
                ["sleep", "100000"],
                network=f"net{i}",
                detach=True,
            )
            client.containers.run(
                "docker.io/library/busybox:latest",
                ["sleep", "100000"],
                network=f"net{i}",
                detach=True,
            )

        before_time = time.time()
        client.networks.list(filters=dict(driver="bridge"))
        return time.time() - before_time

    with dind_factory(
        Dind(
            "quay.io/podman/stable",
            ["podman", "system", "service", "--time=0", "unix:///sockets/docker.sock"],
        )
    ) as unix_socket:
        mocker.patch.dict(os.environ, {"DOCKER_HOST": unix_socket})
        podman_before = time.time()
        podman_execution_time = test()
        print(f"Podman overall setup: {time.time() - podman_before}")

    del os.environ["DOCKER_HOST"]

    with dind_factory(Dind("docker:dind-rootless", None)) as unix_socket:
        mocker.patch.dict(os.environ, {"DOCKER_HOST": unix_socket})
        docker_before = time.time()
        docker_execution_time = test()
        print(f"Docker overall setup: {time.time() - docker_before}")

    assert podman_execution_time <= docker_execution_time
```

Run via `pytest <location of test file>`.  (written with linux in mind, with a local instance of docker running)
It launches podman and docker in containers, creates 25 networks each with 4 containers and monitors how long it takes to list networks (and cleans up after itself).

On my system, this test case takes ~1.5 seconds to list networks via docker compat and 0.003 seconds via docker. 

### Describe the results you received

In the above test case (which in terms of container arrangement is roughly to our software with light usage), listing networks via podman's docker compat layer is far slower than docker and gets disproportionally worse with more networks/containers. 

0.24 seconds vs 0.002 seconds (10 networks, each with 4 containers)
1.5 seconds vs 0.003 seconds (25 networks, each with 4 containers)

### Describe the results you expected

I'd expect podmans's docker network support to be comparable to docker's.

Our issue is that we can create many hundreds of networks with multiple containers each (we're managing IPAM manually to enable this), so podman's docker network listing becomes too slow for us to use and we may have to switch to docker if it can't be sped up. (Python doesn't have an async podman library yet, so switching to podman without the docker company layer isn't an option).

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

We're installing/using podman in an alpine 3.18 privileged container (with fuse-overlayfs).

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
As mention on matrix there is no network -> container mapping in our db structure so we always have to go through all containers to know to what networks they are attached to.

That said reading the code our current complexity is O(C*N) where C is the total number of containers and N the total number of networks (after list filters are applied).  And while C*N is required it is not required to re fetch all containers in each iteration from the db. Fixing that should make things much faster. The other things is we call a full Inspect() in the API endpoint but we really only ever care about the network data  so we can make this simpler as well.

I doubt that we can get as fast as docker without the db structure and caching but at least we should get much closer.
I see a ~62x improvement with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20056 so I hope this is fast enough for your use case.
